items.title: Nose landing gear failure could have caused fatal plane crash, ATSB says

children.content: A plane crash that killed two people and injured several others in Adelaide earlier this year could have been caused by a failure in the plane's nose landing gear, an ongoing investigation has found.

gallery.altText: Smoke coming from buildings behind a fence

thumbnail.altText: Smoke coming from buildings behind a fence

items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on SA coast

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

image.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: SA pushes for AI data centres as industry says it's not asking for subsidies

children.content: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.

image.altText: Masses on cords in a computer server.

items.title: Chihuahua Jet takes to the air as more pets allowed in cabin

children.content: An Australian airline is allowing small cats and dogs on a range of flights, with other passengers appearing unconcerned about sharing the plane with furry creatures.

image.altText: A woman holding a small dog in a carrier at an airport with a sign for Paw-riority boarding

items.title: Melrose Park roadworks bite business as Crafers wombat crossing questioned

children.content: The installation of a raised zebra crossing in Crafers is causing delays for commuters and reducing car parks, while an intersection upgrade in Melrose Park has left a minor arterial route completely blocked off.

image.altText: Detour sign on a suburban road

items.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme

children.content: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

gallery.altText: A close up of a woman with long grey hair, looking off camera

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a woman with long grey hair, looking off camera

items.title: Tony Modra's family 'extremely grateful' for support after crash

children.content: The family of AFL icon Tony Modra has released a statement thanking the community for the "overwhelming show of love" they have received since he was seriously injured in a crash last week.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: Calls to upgrade 'sketchy' Sturt Highway freight route

children.content: Eight councils are calling for urgent safety upgrades to the popular freight and travel route spanning three states.

image.altText: Sturt Highway city distance sign

items.title: Residents worried about dramatic increase to Menindee Lakes water release

children.content: Residents living along the Darling River are worried the decision to double water releases from the Menindee Lakes will remove the system's drought reserve. But authorities say it will help rebuild populations of Murray cod downstream.

image.altText: A sun setting behind clouds at Lake Menindee in outback NSW.

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandmother denies involvement in disappearance

children.content: One of the grandmothers of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont says police have a theory that she buried her grandson after an accident — an allegation she describes as "ludicrous".

image.altText: A person wearing glasses with shoulder-length grey hair

items.title: Delays to alcohol delivery restrictions worry SA anti-violence advocates

children.content: Family violence prevention advocates say the SA government has yet to progress alcohol delivery legislation as recommended by last year's Royal Commission into Domestic, Family and Sexual Violence.

image.altText: a close-up of someone using an iphone to purchase alcohol online

items.title: Russian timber getting into Australia by exploiting loopholes, industry says

children.content: Russian imports have faced tariffs since the start of the war in Ukraine, but timber is being rerouted through third countries and is entering Australia.

image.altText: E nitens logs end on tagged with a distinct number and bar code

items.title: 'We lost our son': India's medical exams scandal leads to tragedy and uproar

children.content: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

gallery.altText: Four women in floral veils have their heads bowed, holding a photo of a young man wearing glasses.

thumbnail.altText: Four women in floral veils have their heads bowed, holding a photo of a young man wearing glasses.

children.content: With billions for health but limited funds for the state's regions, here are the 2026 NSW budget's winners and losers.

image.altText: A graphic of the Australia map in blue with NSW bolded, and gold coins around the map.

items.title: Newly discovered spider uses 'ballista' web to capture prey

children.content: A bizarre species of spider from the rainforests of Far North Queensland springs a nasty surprise.

items.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports

children.content: WiseTech initially gained ground but is now in the red again after Monday's 18 per cent plunge. The ASX is seesawing between gains and losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The blurred side profile of a man walking past a screen that says "ASX welcomes WiseTech Global".

items.title: 'They've lost the plot': UK Labour could learn from Rudd-Gillard soap opera

children.content: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

image.altText: Ken Rudd, wearing a dark suit, gestures with his right hand while holding a pen. Julia Gillard is in the background.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Can Australia still make the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is well underway and it's starting to become clear which teams are progressing through to the next round of competition. But, Australia's fate still isn't sealed.
Australia currently sitting second in its group with one win against Türkiye and one loss against the United States.
We break down how the new qualifying process works, what's changed, and what the Socceroos need from their match on Friday against Paraguay.

items.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

image.altText: Lionel Messi waves to the crowd as he walks off the field after a game

items.title: Photo gives clues to missing man's final hours before disappearance

children.content: A previously unreleased image answers one question in the case of missing Queensland man Jack McLennan, but many others remain.

items.title: 'Like looking into a mirror': I met my siblings at the age of 35

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

image.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

items.title: You won't see Hugh Jackman the same after The Death of Robin Hood

children.content: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

items.title: Quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president

children.content: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

items.title: Award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'

items.title: Australians have record-low trust in the United States, new poll finds

children.content: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

image.altText: Xi Jinping and Donald Trump walk together along the Temple of Heaven.

children.content: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

items.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie is dead, US media reports

children.content: CNN reported that one of the notes claimed that the mother of NBC's Today show host, Savannah Guthrie, was dead, and those who kidnapped her did not mean to kill her, but she died shortly after her disappearance.

items.title: Security agencies issue blunt 'act swiftly' warning on AI

children.content: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

items.title: Victorian farm fined for not meeting free-range standards

children.content: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

image.altText: A cluster of hens crowded towards the camera, blue sky and grass behind.
